summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, git/SSH/cloud account data to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests installer-side identity and account data and POSTs it to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Collected fields include OS hostname, OS username, OS platform/version, cwd, parent project package.json metadata (name/author/repository), ~/.gitconfig user.email, GitHub CLI login/email, SSH public key comment emails enumerated from ~/.ssh/*.pub, git remote origin URL, the last ~50 reflog author emails, GCP project and account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile/SSO identifiers from ~/.aws/config, and /etc/resolv.conf search domain. The package name and peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk impersonate Anthropic's official SDK ecosystem to lure installs; it is not published by Anthropic. Source comments frame the upload as 'anonymous diagnostics' and claim 'no credentials are read', while the code reads ~/.ssh, ~/.aws/config, gcloud properties, and.gitconfig — a cover-story label that does not match the behavior. The harvested data enables targeted follow-on attacks against the developer's GitHub, AWS, and GCP accounts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
